search_candidates.content: ERAL PT Sinar Eka Selaras Tbk Stock Research Report July 17, 2025 PT Ajaib Sekuritas Asia | www.ajaib.co.id 1 This report is intended exclusively for Ajaib Clients. Unauthorized sharing or distribution is strictly forbidden BUY Stock Information (as of July 16, 2025) Stock Performance Comparison vs JCI ERAL’s Shareholders % PT Erajaya Swasembada 80.0 Public 20.0 Shareholders Riding the Retail Shift • Net profit rose 3% YoY to Rp42bn in 1Q25, pressured by rising costs, but revenue grew 25.6% YoY to Rp1.37tn on strong fashion sales. BUY Recommendation with TP at Rp500 We recommend BUY on ERAL with a target price of Rp500, implying a 6.9x 2025F P/E (average peers: 12.0x), supported by its strong multi-segment retail model spanning accessories, fashion, electronics, and the upcoming automotive segment through XPENG EVs. ERAL’s ability to diversify its portfolio and capture shifting consumer trends positions it well for long-term growth, further reinforced by synergies within the Erajaya Group ecosystem and its increasing presence in high-margin branded fashion.

evidence.free_float: Free Float 20.0% Earnings Soft, But Sales Momentum Healthy PT Sinar Eka Selaras Tbk (ERAL) recorded a 3% YoY net profit increase to Rp42bn in 1Q25, despite Share Out. (bn) 5.2 margin headwinds and higher operating costs. Gross profit rose 64% YoY to Rp224bn with a gross margin of 16.4%—a solid improvement from last year, but still under internal benchmarks. Beta 0.7 Operating profit came in at Rp53bn, translating to a 3.8% margin. Bottom-line pressure came from increased opex, losses from the MST Golf joint venture, a higher tax rate (25.6%), and surging interest expenses (+99% YoY). However, top-line remained strong at Rp1.37tn (+25.6% YoY), Stock Performance Comparison vs JCI supported by solid performance in the fashion segment and store rollouts. 70% 60% Fashion Segment Shows Breakout Potential 50% ERAL’s strategic push into branded fashion and active lifestyle retail is yielding results. Fashion 40% 30% apparel sales reached Rp225bn in 1Q25—more than 10x higher than the same period last year— 20% boosting its share of total revenue to 16.4% (from just 2.0%). The segment delivered a healthy 42% 10% gross margin, though operational productivity is still catching up due to early-stage scaling. 0% -10% Accessories and IoT remained the core revenue driver at 70.8%, but margins compressed due to a -20% shift in transfer pricing mechanisms within the group.
